data_in.description: Project JSON document with URL-typed fields

data_out.description: Validation report listing all URL fields containing whitespace, with field names and flagged values

domain_knowledge: URL fields in omics project metadata must not contain whitespace characters to ensure compatibility with data repository links and web application serialization.
Whitespace includes not only visible spaces but also tabs, newlines, and other Unicode space characters that may be invisible during data entry.
JSON schema validation can enforce field-level constraints by defining URL type with pattern matching rules that reject whitespace.
The paired-data-form platform stores project metadata in JSON format, making client-side and server-side validation critical before data persistence.

direct_checks: verify file exists: project JSON document input
verify file_format_is: input is valid JSON
script_runs: validation script executes without error on test inputs
output_matches_reference: validation script identifies all URL fields containing whitespace in a test JSON document with known whitespace-containing URLs, no false negatives
output_matches_reference: validation script produces no false positives on a test JSON document with valid URLs (no whitespace)
contains_substring: validation output includes flagged URL field name(s) and the problematic value(s) containing spaces
expert_review: Confirm URL field schema is correctly identified in the project JSON structure
Confirm whitespace character definition (space, tab, newline, etc.) aligns with platform requirements
Confirm error/warning message is actionable for end users submitting project data

cmd: node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const data = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('{inputs.project_json}', 'utf8')); const report = []; function scanUrls(obj, path = '') { if (typeof obj === 'object' && obj !== null) { for (const key in obj) { const newPath = path ? path + '.' + key : key; if (typeof obj[key] === 'string' && key.toLowerCase().includes('url')) { if (/\s/.test(obj[key])) { report.push({field: newPath, value: obj[key], contains_whitespace: true}); } } else if (typeof obj[key] === 'object') { scanUrls(obj[key], newPath); } } } } scanUrls(data); console.log(JSON.stringify({validation_report: report, total_flagged: report.length, timestamp: new Date().toISOString()}, null, 2)); fs.writeFileSync('{task_id}_validation_report.json', JSON.stringify({validation_report: report, total_flagged: report.length, timestamp: new Date().toISOString()}, null, 2)); process.exit(report.length > 0 ? 1 : 0);"

task_description: Implement a validation rule that scans all URL-typed fields in a project JSON document and flags any value containing whitespace characters, enforcing the constraint that URLs must not include spaces.
task_objective: Implement a validation rule that scans all URL-type fields in project JSON and flags any values containing whitespace characters.
title: Implement URL-space validation for project JSON fields to detect embedded spaces

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: npm, Validation report listing all URL fields containing whitespace, with field names and flagged values.
workflow_description: 1. Load a project JSON document from the platform. 2. Identify all fields designated as URL type in the schema (e.g., project URLs, reference links, data repository links). 3. Iterate through each URL field and check for the presence of whitespace characters (spaces, tabs, newlines). 4. For each URL containing whitespace, record the field name, the offending URL value, and generate a warning message. 5. Compile validation results into a report listing all flagged URLs and their locations within the document.
